# **Software Requirements Specification: Host Listing Management** {#software-requirements-specification:-host-listing-management}

The Listing Management feature allows hosts to seamlessly monitor live marketplace assets, execute inline quick-actions, resume cached onboarding sequences, or launch a multi-tab structural edit workspace to tweak identity criteria, pricing, calendar blockages, and geographic pickup fee indices.

| Attribute | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Actor | Host |
| Trigger | The system shall render Homepage interface when the user triggers Clicking the “My Listings” navigation container |

## **FR-ACS-LIM-1 Listing Overview** {#fr-acs-lim-1-listing-overview}

![][image1]

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.1** The system shall present a text input bar reading exactly "Search fleet by plate, model, or status..."

* Upon focus, it shall accept alphanumeric characters to search for listings by name, plate number, model.

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.2** The system shall display isolated sub-navigation menu allowing hosts to sort current rows via four specific filter tab metrics:

* All Tab: Displays the absolute aggregated total of all vehicles tied to the Host ID.  
* Listed Tab: Limits card generation exclusively to active assets visible to marketplace search engines.  
* Unlisted Tab: Isolates permanent or long-term deactivated vehicle listings.  
* Draft Tab: Displays incomplete onboarding instances waiting for initial submission validation.

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.3 Progressive Incomplete Setup Card:**   
**FR-ACS-LIM-1.3.1** If a record evaluates to an unsubmitted configuration state, the card component shall:

* Display a blue badge to the text: "DRAFT".  
* Enforce a placeholder media backdrop displaying the helper string text: "Setup in progress...".  
* Embed a prominent blue primary action button reading "Complete Setup". Tapping this button instantly loads the multi-step onboarding wizard to its furthest valid state.

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.3.2** If the host has drafted a new vehicle but has not yet uploaded any media assets or setup a specific vehicle make/model configuration during the progressive setup flow:

* The system shall fall back to rendering a hardcoded default system skeleton graphic asset in place of the standard media backdrop wrapper.  
* The system shall replace the standard dynamic title concatenation string with a hardcoded placeholder identity string reading exactly: "New Vehicle".

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.4 Live Asset Interactive Profile Cards**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-1.4.1** For valid active or inactive vehicle records, the dashboard grid shall generate standalone profile cards displaying the structural layouts:

* The Profile Metadata Block: Displays a clean color-coded status indicator chip (Listed \= Light Green, Unlisted \= Light Grey), alongside the vehicle title \[Make\] \+ \[Model\].   
* It must render the lowercase alphanumeric space-trimmed license plate string directly above the calculated flat base daily rate (e.g., "$85/day").

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.4.2** Each valid vehicle profile card must anchor exactly three uniform bottom icon action buttons labeled as: Pricing, Calendar, and Pickup. 

* Clicking any of these individual triggers shall bypass global page routers and link the host straight into that exact contextual sub-tab layout within the master editing suite.

**FR-ACS-LIM-1.5** The system shall display a “+” button to allow hosts to create a new vehicle.

* Clicking this button shall instantly bypass any active dashboard status filters (All, Listed, Unlisted, Draft) and route the host directly into **Step 1 of the vehicle onboarding creation wizard**, initializing a brand new vehicle record instance in a Draft state.

## **FR-ACS-LIM-2 Listing Details Edit** {#fr-acs-lim-2-listing-details-edit}

![][image2]

**FR-ACS-LIM-2.1 Fixed Identity Ledger:** When the host edits an active vehicle profile via the dashboard tab router, the system shall group all foundational tracking elements into a prominent container titled "Vehicle Identification".

* To eliminate catalog fraud, the system shall display the fields **MAKE / MODEL**, **PRODUCTION YEAR**, **LICENSE PLATE**, and **VIN** in a locked read-only text field format.  
* The values must remain unchangeable unless a formal administrative document audit is initiated with platform support specialists.

**FR-ACS-LIM-2.2 Odometer Metric Control:** The system shall expose a specific numeric field layer card titled "Current Mileage (mi)". It allows the host to input an integer representing the car's current total mileage configuration.   
**FR-ACS-LIM-2.3 Description Box:** The system shall anchor a large, text area container titled "Vehicle Description" containing a live inline validation text counter set to a hard-stop ceiling rule of precisely 1,000 characters.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-2.4 Gallery Layout Matrix:** The module titled "Vehicle Photos" shall display a thumbnail tracking string showing active vs capacity metrics (e.g., "7/10 Photos Uploaded"). The first image grid location must display an overlay indicator banner reading exactly "MAIN" to establish the search results hero thumbnail.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-2.5 Binary Features & Custom Chip Array:**

* **System Features:** Renders the core platform feature matrix accompanied by functional toggle switches. Toggling updates the searchable attributes instantaneously.  
* **Custom Features:** Houses an alphanumeric input bar with placeholder text reading exactly "e.g., Bike Rack, Toll Pass" anchored to a blue "+" action box. Committing a entry dynamically appends a dismissible text chip to the sub-tray layout below it (e.g., Child seat included X).

**FR-ACS-LIM-2.6 Confirmation Trigger**

## **FR-ACS-LIM-3 Listing Pricing Edit** {#fr-acs-lim-3-listing-pricing-edit}

![][image3]

**FR-ACS-LIM-3.1 Pricing Data Initialization**

* Upon the host triggering the selection of the "Pricing" sub-tab container, the system shall instantly load and pre-populate all field values (Daily Rate, 3+ Day, 7+ Day, and 30+ Day Trip inputs) into their respective interactive UI blocks.  

**FR-ACS-LIM-3.2 Base Daily Rate Input**

* The system shall provide a text box displaying a prefixed standard $ currency indicator symbol alongside a responsive numerical field used to adjust the baseline 24-hour leasing price point.

**FR-ACS-LIM-3.3 Duration-Based Tier Percentage Reductions:** 

* To encourage extended vehicle booking cycles, the system shall provide a multi-input text block titled "Trip Discounts" that structures incremental percentage deduction metrics across three distinct milestones:  
  * 3+ Day Trip Input: Captures an integer adjusting the percentage discount applied automatically to any booking duration matching or exceeding 3 consecutive calendar days.  
  * 7+ Day Trip Input: Captures an integer adjusting the percentage discount applied automatically to any booking duration matching or exceeding 7 consecutive calendar days.  
  * 30+ Day Trip Input: Captures an integer adjusting the percentage discount applied automatically to any booking duration matching or exceeding 30 consecutive calendar days.  
* Validation Constraint Rules: The input fields shall only accept numeric values between 0 and 99\. 

**FR-ACS-LIM-3.4 Confirmation Trigger**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-3.4.1 "Discard Changes" Button:** Clicking this button purges all uncommitted rate and discount modifications from the temporary workspace cache, reverting fields to their last synchronized database state.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-3.4.2 "Save Settings" Button:** Clicking this button validates formatting and dispatches a single optimization request to commit the pricing parameters permanently to the backend repository.

* The system shall display a green success notification at the top of the viewport reading: "Pricing settings updated successfully." The text fields will refresh to display the newly committed rate and discount percentages.

## **FR-ACS-LIM-4 Listing Availability Edit** {#fr-acs-lim-4-listing-availability-edit}

![][image4]

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.1 Calendar Sheet:**   
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.1.1** Upon the host clicking or navigating to the "Calendar" sub-tab panel wrapper, the interface shall trigger an asynchronous data loading state. 

* The system shall dispatch an optimized, parameterized request fetching the host vehicle's booking schedules, manual overrides, and system constraint blocks for the currently visible month window plus a rolling 1-month forward lookup vector.

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.1.2** While this payload resolves, the 7-column calendar sheet layout framework shall project an animated, desaturated visual skeleton placeholder matrix to indicate active loading and maintain layout stability.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.1.3** Once the data resolves successfully, the skeleton states are instantly replaced by fully functional grid cells populated with their respective database state colors. If a severe system network timeout occurs, the calendar matrix shall drop back to a safe fallback rendering state: locking all editable cells as **Unavailable** and projecting a clear toast notification warning reading: "Failed to synchronize availability schedule. Retrying..."  
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.1.4** The sheet template header displays the active month and year alongside arrow directional control toggles.

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.2 Calendar Cell Color-State Ledger:**   
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.2.1 Cell Color-State Definitions**   
The individual grid cell nodes must parse data models and render colors mapped precisely to their underlying availability tokens:

| Cell State Token | Description | Visual Indicator | Host Mutability | Permitted Interaction / System Rule |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Available** *(Default)* |  | Uncolored / White | **Fully Editable** | Can be selected as an anchor cell for a click-and-drag sequence.  Transitions to **Selected** during interaction and commits to **Blocked** upon clicking the upper action button. |
| **Blocked** | Shows custom unavailable manually locked down by the host to prevent customer booking requests | Solid Red | **Fully Editable** | Can be selected as an anchor cell for a click-and-drag sequence.  Transitions to **Selected** during interaction and reverts to **Available** upon clicking the "Unblock Selected Dates" upper action button. |
| **Selected** | Indicates cells clicked or highlighted by the host during their active selection session | Light Blue | **Volatile Session State** | Represents the active client-side scratchpad selection pool. It is highly mutable during the active gesture but cannot be saved directly until a state transformation button is clicked. |
| **Booked** | Reflects dates reserved by a renter for a confirmed active trip | Light Green | **Strictly Locked (Read-Only)** | Programmatically protected to secure confirmed customer contracts. Bypassed entirely by click-and-drag selections. Cannot be edited, modified, or overwritten by the host under any workflow. |
| **Unavailable** | Indicates dates completely locked out by automated platform constraints  | Solid Grey | **Strictly Locked (Read-Only)** | Enforced by automated system constraints (e.g., the custom 5-day pre/post-trip buffer rules). Completely immutable to the host; cannot be selected or bypassed. |

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.2.2 Automated Pre & Post-Trip Buffer**

* The core duration value (e.g., exactly 5 calendar day) shall be configured by the Platform Administrator via the CMS.    
* Once set, the system backend shall enforce this as an immutable platform rule: whenever a vehicle trip transitions to a confirmed Booked status, the system shall automatically convert both the specified calendar days immediately preceding (BEFORE) and immediately following (AFTER) the reservation block into the Unavailable (Solid Grey) state.   
* This symmetric padding prevents immediate logistical overlaps, accommodates potential late drop-offs, and guarantees an automated 5-day window for the host to inspect, clean, and recharge/refuel the vehicle between bookings. 

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.3 Modification Action Trigger**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.1 Dynamic Volume Calculation:** Click-and-drag actions across editable cells dynamically compute selection volume and update the upper action button.

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.2 Pure-State Selection Constraints:** The initial clicked cell sets an unchangeable session anchor state. Moving the pointer filters selections based on this anchor:

* **FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.2.1 Available Anchor (Blocking):** Selects only adjacent available cells. Pre-existing Blocked, Booked, or Unavailable cells crossed are bypassed.  
* **FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.2.2 Blocked Anchor (Unblocking):** Selects only adjacent Blocked cells. All other cell states crossed are bypassed.  
* **FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.2.3 Read-Only Guardrail:** Initial clicks on Booked or Unavailable cells immediately abort the selection routine.

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.3.3 Dynamic Label Transformation:** Button text dynamically updates based on selection count \[X\] and the anchor state:

* Blocking Configuration: Reads exactly "Block \[X\] Selected Dates". Clicking saves a blocked state to the scratchpad cache.  
* Unblocking Configuration: Reads exactly "Unblock \[X\] Selected Dates". Clicking clears the blocked token in the scratchpad cache.

**FR-ACS-LIM-4.4 Confirmation Trigger**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.4.1 "Discard Changes" Button:** Clicking this shall immediately purge all uncommitted date modifications, clear the current selection scratchpad cache, and revert the grid cells back to their last synchronized database state.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-4.4.2 "Save Settings" Button:** Clicking this shall collect all pending date status changes (e.g., custom date blocks added during the session), compile them into a unified payload, and dispatch a single optimization request to the vehicle repository backend to commit the changes permanently to the live asset listing.

* Upon successful processing of the confirmation action payload, the system locks active field interactions temporarily, commits the database payload, and projects a green success viewport toast reading: "Calendar settings updated successfully."

## **FR-ACS-LIM-5 Listing Pickup Locations Edit** {#fr-acs-lim-5-listing-pickup-locations-edit}

![][image5]

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.1 Pickup Location Data Initialization**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.1.1** Upon the host triggering the selection of the "Pickup" sub-tab panel wrapper , the system shall dispatch an authenticated asynchronous request to fetch the fleet location pickup profiles.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.1.2** During payload resolution, the pickup container shall render a skeleton loading structural layout to ensure interface continuity.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.1.3** Once resolved, the system maps the active location arrays. If the asset does not possess any configured custom drop-off locations, the view state shall default to displaying only the vehicle's registration home base address tagged with the "Primary" structural badge.

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.2 Pickup Hub Overview**   
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.2.1**The sub-tab titled **Pickup** shall render a structured list detailing all delivery points currently configured for the specific asset profile.   
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.2.2** The card shell anchors a right-aligned utility link button labeled precisely "+ Add Custom" to append new location search nodes to the listing's delivery map.  
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.2.3** Each delivery point item container displayed within the layout framework must render data:

* Location Title: Displays the primary geographic destination  
* Hand-off Instructions: Renders descriptive sub-text mapping out specific terminals or pickup zones directly beneath the primary title layout block.  
* Primary Inventory Anchor Token: If a specific location record represents the vehicle's permanent home address, the interface shall append a solid blue status badge reading exactly "Primary" adjacent to the Location Title element.  
* Pickup fee: Renders the active delivery fee calculated for that specific destination 

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3 Location Editing**   
**![][image6]**  
**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.1 Inline Dropdown Expansion Block:** Clicking the inline edit icon tool adjacent to any existing pickup location row item shall dynamically toggle the expansion of an integrated fields input drawer positioned directly below the specific location card component. 

The expanded panel layout must render five key functional inputs:

* Geographic Address Input (Address search string)  
* Interactive Map Node (Visual map pinpoint viewport)  
* Pickup Fee Input (Numeric field prefixed with a $ currency symbol)  
* Hand-off Instructions Textbox (Rich-text container for custom host guidelines)

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.2 Immutable Location Lock Rules:** To prevent host delivery bait-and-switch schemes and protect renter trip confirmations, the system shall evaluate whether a specific location has been actively utilized (i.e., it is linked to a past, current, or upcoming confirmed Booked reservation):

* **If Location Has Been Used:** The system shall permanently freeze the core geographic attributes. The Geographic Address Input, and Interactive Map Node components must transition to an immutable, disabled read-only view state. The host shall not be permitted to modify the physical location address.  
* **Permissible Adjustments:** While the address fields are locked, the host remains authorized to modify the operational criteria: the Delivery Fee Input and the Hand-off Instructions Textbox remain fully interactive and editable.  
* **Hide Location:** If a host wishes to deprecate a locked/frozen geographic destination, the layout must provide a dismiss/remove checkbox button wrapper. Activating this flag immediately flags the record as inactive, hiding it entirely from future marketplace search results while keeping past booking data structures intact..

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.3 Button Action** 

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.3.1 "Cancel Edit" Button:** Clicking this shall immediately collapse the inline drawer component, wipe any uncommitted alphanumeric modifications made within the input fields from local memory cache, and gracefully restore the row item back to its default read-only summary state view layout.

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.3.2 "Update Location" Button:** Clicking this shall validate all field formats (e.g., confirming the currency mask values inside the Pickup Fee ($) input field). 

* Once local interface assertions pass, it caches the updated local attributes (*Pickup Fee, Hand-off Instructions, Hide Toggle Status*) into the row context dataset before collapsing the panel.  
* The system closes the input tray stream gracefully, and projects a green success viewport toast reading exactly: "Pickup locations updated successfully."

**FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.4 Add Pickup Locations**

Clicking "+ Add Location" appends a totally clean, fresh version of the Dropdown Expansion Drawer (FR-ACS-LIM-5.3.1) into the active screen viewport.

## **FR-ACS-LIM-6 Listing Status Control**  {#fr-acs-lim-6-listing-status-control}

**FR-ACS-LIM-6.1** The system shall display a persistent status toggle switch directly within the sub-header viewport tier, consistently aligned across the Details, Pricing, Calendar, and Pickup configuration workspace sheets.

**FR-ACS-LIM-6.2** Clicking the toggle instantly launches a modal confirmation dialogue box overlaying the active viewport before any state change executes.

**FR-ACS-LIM-6.3 Action Routing:**

* Confirming "Change Status": Clicking the primary button inside the modal commits the change directly to the production database.  
* Canceling: Clicking cancel aborts the transaction, closes the modal, and leaves the toggle unchanged.

**FR-ACS-LIM-6.4 Post-Commit Updates:**

* Active State ("Listed"): Sub-header updates to a light green badge reading exactly "Listed". The vehicle asset goes live on the marketplace search engine.  
  * The system triggers a green success toast notification at the top viewport tier reading exactly: "Listing is now active on the marketplace.   
* Inactive State ("Unlisted"): Sub-header updates to a light grey badge reading exactly "Unlisted". The asset is hidden from active searches, while active or upcoming user trips remain protected from cancellation.  
  * The system triggers a neutral informational toast notification at the top viewport tier reading exactly: "Listing deactivated. Existing upcoming bookings remain protected."
